steps.title: Find the other acute angle
steps.content:
In triangle $ABC$, angle $B$ is $90^\circ$ and angle $ACB$ is $58^\circ$. The two acute angles must complete the triangle's $180^\circ$ angle sum:
$$
BAC=180^\circ-90^\circ-58^\circ=32^\circ
$$

steps.title: Use the parallel segment
steps.content:
Since $RS$ is parallel to $AB$, angle $CRS$ corresponds to angle $CAB$. Corresponding angles formed by parallel lines are equal, so
$$
CRS=32^\circ
$$

steps.title: Use the linear pair
steps.content: Points $A$, $R$, and $C$ lie on the same line, so angles $ARS$ and $CRS$ form a linear pair. Their measures sum to $180^\circ$: $$ ARS=180^\circ-32^\circ=148^\circ $$ The answer is 148.
